title: podman system high CPU usage when using Docker CLI
body:
### Issue Description

Hi there. We have been observing high CPU usage (between 1 and 1.5 cores) from the `podman system service` process, even when running no particularly intensive workload.
We tried to debug whether we were using some anomalous options when running the containers, and finally I managed to figure out that the root cause seem to be the usage of the Docker CLI to interact with the containerizer socket. 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Use Ubuntu 22.04, and install podman and the docker CLI from Canonical upstream (should be v3.4.4 and v20.10.12 respectively)
2. Start the podman system service.
3. Open a couple terminal windows, one with htop sorted by CPU usage
4. Run `DOCKER_HOST=unix://$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/podman/podman.sock docker run --rm ubuntu:jammy sleep 10`, and observe high CPU usage from the podman system process
5. Run `podman run --remote --rm docker.io/ubuntu:jammy sleep 10`, and observe no CPU usage anomaly


### Describe the results you received

The APIs invoked by the Docker CLI cause higher CPU usage.

### Describe the results you expected

I would expect the Podman and the Docker CLI to have similar outcomes when interacting with the podman service socket.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

comments.body:
Please test this with the lastest podman version (4.7). We do not support older versions upstream and 3.4.4 is very old.
I also cannot reproduce this with your command against the latest version so I assume it was fixed already.

For anyone still encountering this issue on podman versions provided with the current Ubuntu releases, I did some more debugging myself, and this is the patch that solves it -> https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/18149
